front: Philosopher: satirized Leibniz's Theodicy via the character Pangloss, who insists we live in the 'best of all possible worlds'
Work: discusses the Quakers, Isaac Newton, and the trial of Charles I; its author had to flee Paris after praising inoculation
back: Philosophical Letters / Letters on the English (Voltaire)

front: Work: the 12th letter praises Francis Bacon as the 'father of experimental philosophy'
Work: the 25th letter discusses but rejects Pascal's theological approach to the problem of evil
Philosopher: popularized the myth of Newton's apple and championed Newton's rational thought over Descartes' imagination
Work: published anonymously in Geneva by Gabriel Grasset; similar form to a work by Bayle; argues for deism against the Judeo-Christian God
Philosopher: used the case of Jean Calas, an innocent Calvinist killed on the wheel, to argue for religious tolerance
Philosopher: wrote 'if God did not exist, it would be necessary to invent him' in a commentary on The Three Imposters, arguing belief in an afterlife prevents criminal behavior
Philosopher: Mikhail Bakunin inverted this thinker's famous quote to say 'If God really existed, it would be necessary to abolish him'
Philosopher: imagines the 'obstinate pride' needed to say 'only my anthill is beloved of God' among 'nine hundred million little ants'
Philosopher: argued that China had 'perfected moral science' and advocated integrating Chinese social structures into Europe
Philosopher: Essay on the Universal History, the Manners, and the Spirit of the Nations is credited with founding social history
